name: Stumptown Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Direct trade relationships with farms in over 10 countries
description: One of the pioneers of the third-wave coffee movement, Stumptown built deep direct-trade relationships with farms before it was fashionable. Their Hair Bender espresso remains a benchmark.

name: Intelligentsia
sourcing: Direct Trade pioneer
description: A pioneer of Direct Trade, Intelligentsia works closely with farmers to elevate coffee quality and sustainability.

name: Blue Bottle Coffee
sourcing: Focus on peak freshness
description: Started in Oakland, Blue Bottle is known for its rigorous standards for freshness and meticulous brewing methods.

name: Counter Culture Coffee
sourcing: Sustainable and educational focus
description: Dedicated to sustainability and coffee education, Counter Culture sets the standard for environmentally friendly roasting.

name: George Howell Coffee
sourcing: Terroir-focused, freezing green coffee
description: Founded by a specialty coffee legend, George Howell focuses on capturing the purest terroir, often freezing green coffee to preserve freshness.

name: Square Mile Coffee
sourcing: Seasonal direct and relationship sourcing, focusing on traceability and quality lots
description: Co-founded by James Hoffmann, Square Mile is one of the most influential specialty roasters in the world. Based in East London since 2008, they've shaped the vocabulary of third-wave coffee with meticulously sourced seasonal lots, transparent tasting notes, and a commitment to education.

name: Has Bean Coffee
sourcing: Experimental and direct relationships
description: Known for its quirky branding and incredibly vast selection of single-origin coffees, often highlighting experimental processing.

name: The Barn
sourcing: Uncompromising quality, light roasts
description: A vanguard of the German specialty scene, The Barn is famous for its strict, unblended light roasts that showcase origin flavors.

name: Five Elephant
sourcing: Direct trade, relationship coffee
description: Renowned for their exceptional direct trade coffees and their famously delicious cheesecake.

name: Koppi
sourcing: Long-term farm relationships in Costa Rica, Colombia, Ethiopia
description: A classic Nordic roastery offering beautifully clean and bright coffees with a strong focus on terroir and long-term producer partnerships.

name: Tim Wendelboe
sourcing: Own farm project in Honduras, long-standing relationships in Colombia, Ethiopia, Kenya
description: Run by the World Barista Champion, this roastery is famous for its obsessive approach to quality, including soil management at the farm level. Archetype of the Nordic light roast.

name: Fuglen
sourcing: Nordic style
description: A historic vintage design shop turned legendary espresso bar and roastery, defining the Oslo coffee aesthetic.

name: Onibus Coffee
sourcing: Connecting people through coffee
description: Taking its name from the Portuguese word for 'bus', Onibus focuses on coffee as a vehicle to connect people, offering sweet and balanced roasts.

name: Streamer Coffee
sourcing: Focus on milk drinks and latte art
description: Founded by a Free Pour Latte Art Champion, Streamer is famous for its rich espresso blends designed specifically to pair beautifully with milk.

name: Proud Mary Coffee
sourcing: High-end farms from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia, Panama; highlights individual farms and processing
description: A cornerstone of Melbourne's world-leading coffee culture, known for securing exclusive and highly experimental microlots from top farms globally.

name: Sample Coffee
sourcing: Approachable specialty
description: Focused on creating approachable, consistently delicious coffees that fit seamlessly into daily routines.

name: Color Roasters
sourcing: Modern specialty
description: Bringing the vibrant, acid-forward third-wave coffee style to the historic streets of Istanbul.

name: Lot Forty One
sourcing: Microlots
description: A boutique roastery in Istanbul focused on transparently sourced microlots.

name: Café Saint-Henri
sourcing: Direct trade, cooperative focus
description: A pioneering force in Quebec's specialty coffee scene, known for its elegant cafes and meticulously sourced coffees.

name: Heart Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Uncompromising quality
description: Famous for their extremely rigorous quality control and beautiful, cleanly developed light roasts.

name: Sightglass Coffee
sourcing: Direct relationships
description: An independent, sibling-owned coffee company known for its stunning flagship roastery and highly curated coffees.

name: Ritual Coffee
sourcing: Pioneering direct trade
description: One of the original instigators of the third-wave movement in San Francisco, changing the way people thought about coffee.

name: Verve Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Farmlevel initiative
description: Combining coastal California surf culture with rigorous 'Farmlevel' sourcing practices.

name: Elm Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Light and bright
description: A modern Seattle roastery pushing back against the city's dark-roast history with vibrant, light, and sweet coffees.

name: Onyx Coffee Lab
sourcing: Extreme transparency, competition lots from Ethiopia, Colombia, Honduras, Panama
description: Known for their edgy branding, extreme pricing transparency, and dominance in US coffee competitions. One of the most-hyped roasters in the USA.

name: Rave Coffee
sourcing: Ethically sourced single origins, direct relationships with farmers and cooperatives worldwide
notable_coffees: Costa Rica Monte Alto Venecia Nº 348
Sebastián Ramírez Pink Bourbon Session IPA
description: Founded in 2012 in the Cotswolds market town of Cirencester, Rave Coffee has become one of the UK's most respected specialty roasters. Their numbered single origin series spans the globe — from natural Tanzanian lots to experimental anaerobic Colombians fermented with hops.

name: Origin Coffee
sourcing: Direct-trade coffees from Brazil, Nicaragua, El Salvador, Colombia and other Latin American and African producers
description: One of the UK's most respected roasters, with roots in Cornwall and a strong London presence. Origin works on long direct-trade relationships with producers across Latin America and Africa, roasting light to medium for filter and espresso.

name: Monmouth Coffee
sourcing: Long-term farm relationships in Latin America and East Africa
description: A London institution since 1978, Monmouth helped define the British specialty coffee scene before 'third wave' was a term. Medium-light, sweet, and approachable — less extreme acidity than Nordic-style roasts, more classic quality.

name: Ozone Coffee
sourcing: Direct-trade lots from Central/South America and Africa
description: Originating in New Zealand before launching in London, Ozone is café-friendly and modern — light-medium roasts that work beautifully in milk-based drinks and as bright single origins.

name: Dark Arts Coffee
sourcing: Rotating single origins from Central/South America and Africa, often individual farms and washing stations
description: London's most theatrical specialty roaster. Dark Arts wraps premium naturals and experimental processes in striking occult-inspired branding. Modern specialty, punchy and flavour-forward, with lots of naturals.

name: Round Hill Roastery
sourcing: Traceable single farms and washing stations in Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia and other high-altitude origins
description: Scandi-leaning Somerset roastery with very light, high-clarity roasts especially suited to filter. Detailed producer info accompanies every release.

name: Colonna Coffee
sourcing: High-end micro-lot coffees from Colombia, Ethiopia, Panama and other competition origins
description: Competition-calibre specialty roaster from Bath. Maxwell Colonna-Dashwood (World Brewer's Cup finalist) runs an operation dedicated to very light, aroma-forward roasts for precise espresso and filter.

name: Kiss the Hippo
sourcing: Organic coffees from Honduras, Colombia, Ethiopia, naming specific farms or cooperatives
description: London's first certified organic and carbon-neutral specialty roaster. Clean, modern light roasts with a strong sustainability focus and a loyal following.

name: North Star Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Close producer relationships across Brazil, Colombia, Rwanda, Kenya
description: Leeds-based and independently owned, North Star has built a reputation for sweet, balanced medium-light roasts and deep, long-term producer relationships. A cornerstone of the northern UK specialty scene.

name: Horsham Coffee Roaster
sourcing: Direct-trade links in Costa Rica, Rwanda, Kenya and Brazil
description: A small West Sussex roastery with punching-above-its-weight single origins and tight ethical sourcing. Direct links with farms in Costa Rica and East Africa.

name: Clifton Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Ethical sourcing from Kenya, Panama, Brazil, Colombia, El Salvador, Ethiopia
description: Bristol's leading specialty roaster, known for fresh seasonal lots, light-medium roasts, and strong commitments to ethical sourcing and community training.

name: Petra Roasting Co.
sourcing: Specialty Arabica from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia, Brazil; highlights individual farms and washing stations
description: One of Istanbul's leading third-wave roasters. Light to medium-light with lots of single origins — strong focus on filter and espresso in the Scandi direction.

name: Probador Colectiva
sourcing: Micro-lots from Colombia, Ethiopia, Kenya, Panama with detailed producer information
description: High-end Istanbul specialty roaster offering very light, complex tasting-menu style coffees and limited lots. One of the most ambitious roasters in Turkey.

name: Kronotrop
sourcing: Rotating single origins from Colombia, Brazil, Ethiopia, traceable to specific farms or co-ops
description: One of the first specialty micro-roasters in Turkey. Light-medium roasts, balanced and accessible for both espresso and filter — a pioneer of Istanbul's third-wave scene.

name: Montag Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Single origins from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia, Brazil varying seasonally by farm
description: Third-wave specialty roaster based in Kadıköy with an emphasis on clean, modern profiles. Light-medium roasts popular in Istanbul's hip neighbourhoods.

name: Coffee Department
sourcing: Coffees from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia, Brazil presented as single farms or small producer groups
description: Specialty roaster-café in Balat with contemporary light-medium profiles and lots of filter options. Part of the wave of roasters that put Istanbul on the global specialty map.

name: Norm Coffee
sourcing: African and Latin American single origins from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia with farm or mill names
description: Very third-wave café-roaster in Cihangir. Lighter roasts, fruity and aromatic, especially for pour-over. A favourite in Istanbul's specialty café scene.

name: Kava Coffee Roasting
sourcing: Single origins from Central/South America and East Africa rotating with farms and cooperatives
description: Specialty roaster in Moda with approachable light-medium profiles, balancing espresso-friendly blends with quality single origin filter offerings.

name: Kimma Roasters
sourcing: Single-origin beans from Ethiopia, Colombia, Brazil with farm traceability
description: Smaller Istanbul specialty roaster praised for genuinely fresh roasts and clear origin character. Generally light-medium with transparent sourcing.

name: Kurukahveci Mehmet Efendi
sourcing: Arabica beans from various origins including Yemen and Arabian Peninsula; now multi-origin blends
description: One of the oldest and most iconic coffee roasters in Turkey, founded in 1871. Darker roast, extremely fine grind, designed for cezve/ibrik. A cultural institution in the Grand Bazaar district.

name: Kahve Dünyası
sourcing: Blends of beans from Brazil and other origins for consistent house profiles
description: Turkey's largest specialty coffee chain, now with hundreds of locations globally. Medium to dark roasts for Turkish coffee and espresso. More commercial than third-wave but a significant part of Turkish coffee culture.

name: Seven Seeds
sourcing: Traceable single origins from Colombia, Guatemala, Ethiopia, Kenya naming specific farms or co-ops
description: One of Melbourne's most influential specialty roasters. Light-medium, clean and structured — perfect for Melbourne café culture. Strong emphasis on single origin transparency and traceability.

name: Market Lane Coffee
sourcing: Direct relationships with farms in Brazil, Guatemala, Colombia, Rwanda, Ethiopia
description: Very transparent sourcing, light-medium roasts, often on the lighter side for filter but still friendly for espresso. Spotlights individual producers and mills rather than generic regions.

name: St ALi
sourcing: Brazil, Colombia, Ethiopia, Honduras; named farms and long-term partners
description: A Melbourne institution that helped launch Australia's specialty coffee scene. Medium-light but bolder and more comfort-oriented — designed to shine in milk-based drinks as well as black.

name: Five Senses Coffee
sourcing: Direct trade across Central/South America, Africa and Asia; farm-level projects and community work
description: Perth-founded with strong Melbourne presence, Five Senses is a leading force in Australian specialty coffee education and sourcing. Light-medium, very balanced, with a big focus on consistent espresso blends alongside single origins.

name: Single O
sourcing: Rotating single origins from Ethiopia, Kenya, Burundi, Colombia; farm/mill traceability and seasonal purchasing
description: One of Sydney's most important specialty roasters. Lighter, fruit-forward and bright — especially for filters — but still diallable for espresso. Strong emphasis on traceability and seasonal purchasing.

name: ONA Coffee
sourcing: Micro-lots from Colombia, Ethiopia, Panama; anaerobic, natural and experimental processes
description: Competition-driven and highly acclaimed, ONA is one of Australia's most ambitious specialty roasters. Very light with lots of experimental and high-scoring lots from top competition origins.

name: Code Black Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Single origins from Brazil, Colombia, Ethiopia identified by farm or co-operative and harvest season
description: Medium-light specialty roaster with rich, chocolatey-leaning blends for milk and brighter single origins for filter. A respected part of Melbourne's dense specialty scene.

name: Industry Beans
sourcing: Seasonal single origins from Colombia, Peru, Ethiopia with transparent farm and processing information
description: Light-medium, very clean and modern Melbourne roastery. Lots of attention to filter and cold brew profiles as well as espresso. Known for creative café experiences and high-quality retail.

name: Padre Coffee
sourcing: Beans from Brazil, Colombia, Ethiopia; named farms or regional co-ops
description: Medium-light specialty roaster with very approachable flavour. Blends are a big hit with home espresso drinkers, plus accessible single origins. A beloved part of Melbourne's café culture.

name: Juan Valdez Café
sourcing: 100% Colombian Arabica from farmer co-operatives across all major Colombian growing regions
description: The iconic face of Colombian coffee worldwide. Juan Valdez is owned by the National Federation of Coffee Growers of Colombia (FNC), representing over 500,000 farming families. Their cafés and roasting operation promote premium Colombian regional coffees globally, from the high-altitude slopes of Nariño to the classic Eje Cafetero.

name: Café San Alberto
sourcing: Estate coffee from their own family farm in Buenavista, Quindío — specific hacienda lots
description: A premium estate roaster in the heart of Colombia's Coffee Axis (Eje Cafetero). Polished medium-roast specialty coffees from their family-run hacienda in Quindío — one of the most-visited coffee tourism destinations in Colombia.

name: Amor Perfecto
sourcing: Direct work with producers across Huila, Nariño, Tolima; single-farm and community lots
description: One of Bogotá's most respected modern specialty roasters. Light-medium roasts compared to supermarket coffee, designed for both filter and espresso. Champions direct relationships with Colombian farmers.

name: Azahar Coffee
sourcing: Single-origin coffees from Huila, Nariño, Cauca and the Coffee Axis with detailed farm and producer information
description: Third-wave specialty roaster based in Bogotá. Usually medium-light, clean and traceable, popular in high-end cafés across Colombia. Strong reputation for origin transparency.

name: Libertario Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Farms around Huila and Tolima; often releasing microlots from individual fincas
description: Light-medium specialty roaster with a strong focus on origin character and brewing methods. Works directly with finca owners in Colombia's southern highlands.

name: Tropicalia
sourcing: Small producers in Huila, Nariño and Cauca; special process lots with clearly identified farms
description: Contemporary specialty shop-roaster in Bogotá. Lighter roasts with fruit-forward, experimental profiles. Focus on small-lot Colombian producers with alternative processing methods.

name: Café Sello Rojo
sourcing: Large-scale blends from Antioquia, Caldas, Huila — 100% Colombian Arabica
description: One of Colombia's most iconic household coffee brands, beloved as the everyday tinto. Founded in 1920 and roasted in Medellín, Sello Rojo is Colombia's coffee culture in a cup — bold, medium-dark, and always present at the breakfast table.

name: April Coffee
sourcing: Competition-level lots from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia with experimental processing
description: Very light, ultra-precise Nordic style roasting. High clarity, often best as filter — one of the most admired roasters in the Scandinavian specialty scene. Competition-driven sourcing at its finest.

name: Archers Coffee
sourcing: Top Ethiopian, Kenyan and Colombian microlots; COE and auction coffees
description: One of the Middle East's most highly regarded specialty roasters. Very light to light-medium, competition and rare-lot focused. Bringing serious Nordic-style specialty coffee to the Gulf region.

name: Coffee Collective
sourcing: Long-term direct partnerships in Kenya, Ethiopia, Guatemala with detailed farm transparency
description: A benchmark Nordic light roast. Bright, transparent, sweet — Coffee Collective helped define what Scandinavian specialty coffee means internationally. Long-term producer relationships are the cornerstone of their work.

name: Drop Coffee
sourcing: Washed East African and Central American coffees from specific farms and cooperatives
description: Classic Nordic roastery producing very light, aromatic coffees with high acidity. Mostly washed East African and Central American lots from specific farms. A staple of the Stockholm specialty scene.

name: Gardelli Specialty Coffees
sourcing: Rare and high-scoring lots from Latin America and East Africa, named producers and farms
description: Italy's foremost light-roast specialty roaster and multiple World Roasting Champion. Fruit-forward, complex and competition-style — breaking Italy's dark-roast tradition with exceptional precision.

name: La Cabra
sourcing: Experimental and washed lots from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia, Costa Rica — often farm-specific
description: Very light, tea-like and super high clarity — best as pour-over. La Cabra has become internationally acclaimed for their uncompromising Nordic approach and thoughtful design.

name: Sey Coffee
sourcing: Washed and experimental lots from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia tied to a specific farm or washing station
description: Very light and ultra-transparent — one of the most hyped modern filter roasters in the USA. Each bag is tied to a specific farm or washing station. Minimal aesthetic, maximum coffee quality.

name: Sweet Bloom Coffee Roasters
sourcing: Direct relationships with producers in Colombia, Honduras, Ethiopia
description: Light-medium, very sweet and approachable but still clearly third-wave. Based in Colorado with a strong commitment to direct producer relationships and farm-level transparency.

name: Standout Coffee
sourcing: COE, BOP and competition microlots from Ethiopia, Colombia, Panama
description: Ultra high-end, competition-driven Stockholm roaster. Very light, often 'wow' coffees at premium prices. Specialises in Cup of Excellence and Best of Panama auction lots.

name: Bonanza Coffee
sourcing: African and Latin American single origins, farm- and co-op-specific
description: Light-medium European specialty with a slightly more approachable profile than the most extreme Nordics. Bonanza is one of Berlin's most established third-wave roasters with a loyal international following.

name: Man vs Machine
sourcing: Rotating single origins from Ethiopia, Kenya, Colombia with attention to processing variety
description: Light-medium, punchy modern specialty from Munich. One of Germany's most respected independent roasters, bringing Nordic-influenced precision to Bavaria.

name: Substance
sourcing: High-end Ethiopian, Kenyan and Colombian coffees with detailed producer info
description: Light, intensely aromatic and frequently cited as one of Paris's best specialty roasters. Substance challenges France's traditional dark-roast culture with extreme clarity and precision roasting.
